Details: Aeration processes, in general, rely on the diffusion of contaminants from the treated water to non-contaminated air. These technologies enhance the natural tendency of a volatile organic compound to migrate from a liquid phase to a gas phase in order to achieve equilibrium by increasing air-to-water contact. Because the volatility of 1,2,3-trichloropropane is relatively low, aeration processes must have high air-to-water ratios to achieve contaminant removal.
